mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0% and remains elevated for 2+ consecutive months, the restrictive monetary stance increases borrowing costs and reduces equity valuations through higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive rates above neutral
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months signals labor market deterioration and recession risk, triggering equity risk-off behavior and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite UNRATE deterioration, or correlation between UNRATE change and forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) above 1,400 thousand units signals robust residential investment and consumer confidence, indicating sustained aggregate demand growth that supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,356.0 thousand units as of 2024-08-15
failure_condition: Rank IC between HOUST and 20-day forward returns is < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or SPX produces negative returns in > 55% of months with HOUST > 1,400.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining below 102.5 index points signals supply-side weakness and reduced corporate earnings capacity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice growth expectations downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances when INDPRO < 102.5, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) both decline simultaneously by more than 2% month-over-month, signaling a synchronized demand shock across consumer and residential sectors that triggers equity selloffs and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 2% MoM in same month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 710,773M and HOUST at 1,356K as of 2024-08-15
regime_hint: demand-shock environment or early recession signal
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of co-declining instances, or the joint effect of RSAFS and HOUST declines is not statistically significant (interaction p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 100 thousand persons per month while unemployment rate (UNRATE) remains stable or rises, indicating labor market softening that constrains consumer spending and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change < 100K persons AND UNRATE >= prior month
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 159,105K and UNRATE at 4.1% as of 2024-09-15
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the joint signal of weak payroll growth and rising unemployment does not predict negative returns significantly better than either signal alone (interaction F-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises above 315 index points and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains below 5.5%, the real interest rate becomes negative, signaling monetary accommodation that supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 314.686 and FEDFUNDS at 5.13% as of 2024-09-15
regime_hint: negative real rates environment supporting risk assets
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction between inflation and policy rates does not significantly improve prediction accuracy over single-variable models (R² improvement < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly shows acceleration above 2.5% annualized rate, signaling robust economic expansion that supports corporate earnings and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days in the subsequent quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 23,223.906 billion chained 2017 dollars as of 2024-04-15
regime_hint: mid-to-late cycle expansion with above-trend growth
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 growth rate and 60-day forward returns is < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or SPX produces negative returns in > 55% of quarters with GDPC1 growth > 2.5% annualized.
